Title: Neuroinflammation in neurodegenerative diseases: pathogenic pathways and emerging pharmacotherapeutic targets in Alzheimer's and Parkinson's disease.
Abstract_Summary: This chronic brain inflammation leads to synaptic dysfunction, neuronal loss and ultimately clinical deterioration. Clinical trials targeting microglial phenotypes, cytokine signaling, inflammasome activity, and genetic risk factors are emerging therapeutic strategies.

Title: The Protective Effects of Small-Molecule Compound 0242 Against LPS-Induced Neuroinflammation and in P301S Tau Transgenic Mice.
Abstract_Summary: Neuroinflammation and tau pathology are central drivers of Alzheimer's disease (AD) progression, necessitating multi-target therapeutic strategies. In lipopolysaccharide (LPS)-stimulated BV-2 microglia, 0242 treatment significantly inhibited cell activation and nitric oxide release without cytotoxicity, while downregulating the mRNA levels of pro-inflammatory cytokines IL-1β and TNF-α.

Title: From DNA-encoded library (DEL) screening to in vivo validation: LILRB4 (ILT3)-targeted small molecules reprograms myeloid immune suppression.
Abstract_Summary: Alzheimer's disease (AD) remains a major unmet clinical challenge, with limited therapeutic strategies capable of effectively modulating neuroimmune dysfunction. In human iPSC-derived microglia, APX1 suppressed SHP1/2 phosphorylation, attenuated NF-κB activation and IL-1β secretion, and restored Aβ42 uptake under ApoE-driven inflammatory conditions.

Title: Senescent cells accumulate lipid droplets.
Abstract_Summary: Senescent cells (SnCs) are growth-arrested yet remain metabolically active and undergo extensive reprogramming to support their survival and the Senescence-Associated Secretory Phenotype (SASP). SnCs undergo key metabolic changes, including increased glycolysis, altered mitochondrial function and dysregulated lipid metabolism.

Title: Vorinostat Rescues Cognitive Deficits in a Neuroinflammatory Mouse Model: A Study of Sex Differences and the Underlying TLR4/NF-κB Mechanism.
Abstract_Summary: Western blot and quantitative PCR (qPCR) were used to detect hippocampal expression of the TLR4/TRAF6/IKKα/NF-κB pathway, inflammatory factors (IL-6, IL-1β, TNF-α, iNOS), and neuroplasticity-related proteins (BDNF, p-CREB). SAHA treatment markedly alleviated LPS-induced inflammation, neuropathology, and cognitive deficits.

Title: Cholinergic regulation of neuroinflammation: linking microglia, immunometabolism, and neuromodulation.
Abstract_Summary: However, its neurological relevance extends beyond the classical peripheral vagus nerve-mediated inflammatory reflex. Within the central nervous system, cholinergic signaling interacts with resident immune cells, particularly microglia, and influences inflammatory tone, neuronal vulnerability, and tissue repair.

Title: Inflammatory alterations mediate tau-associated neurodegeneration.
Abstract_Summary: Cognitive assessments and consensus diagnoses were performed at the Columbia Alzheimer's Disease Research Center with biomarker information when available to define cognitive impairment. Exploratory analyses were stratified by amyloid positivity.

Title: Fus-depleted oligodendrocytes reduce neuronal damage and Alzheimer's disease progression in the AppNL-G-F mouse.
Abstract_Summary: In normal brain homeostatic conditions, OL maintain neuronal health through myelin axon interactions and by supplying neurotrophic and metabolic support. We evaluated microglia density and state, astrocytic activation and toxic phenotype, myelin density, cholesterol content, amyloid plaque burden, presynaptic structures, and neuronal hypoxic and oxidative damage in the hippocampus and cortex.

Title: Zika virus infection induces a persistent accumulation of Alzheimer's disease-like Tau phosphorylation in adult immunocompetent mice in association with memory and social behavior impairments.
Abstract_Summary: Clinical and epidemiological data support the link between viral encephalitis and neurodegeneration but its causal mechanism remains mostly unknown. Zika virus (ZIKV) is an emerging, neurotropic flavivirus susceptible to induce cognitive impairments in infected adults.

Title: EXPRESS: Toll Like Receptor 4 (TLR4) in Neuroinflammation: From Acute Hemorrhagic Stroke to Chronic Neurodegeneration.
Abstract_Summary: While the primary injury is defined by mechanical tissue disruption, the progression of secondary brain injury is largely driven by robust neuroinflammatory cascades initiated by blood derived damage-associated molecular patterns (DAMPs). Emerging therapeutic strategies targeting TLR4 and its downstream signaling components including small molecules, natural compounds, aptamers, and TLR4-Lyn interaction modulators demonstrate promising potential in attenuating neuroinflammation and improving neurological outcomes.
